Question: Find $$\text{div} \mathbf{\text{F}}$$ for $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y , z ) = \langle x y , 5 - z^{2} y , x^{2} + y^{2} \rangle .$$
Let $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle \text{unicode[:-]} a y , b x \rangle$$ be a rotational field where a and b are positive constants. Is F source free?

For vector field $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( x , y ) = \langle \text{unicode[:-]} x y , y \rangle , y > 0 ,$$ find all points P such that the amount of fluid flowing in to P equals the amount of fluid flowing out of P .
Find the curl of $$\mathbf{\text{F}} = \langle \text{sin} x \text{cos} z , \text{sin} y \text{sin} z , \text{cos} x \text{cos} y \rangle$$ at point $$( 0 , \frac{\pi}{2} , \frac{\pi}{2} ) .$$

Field $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( x , y ) = \langle - \frac{y}{x^{2} + y^{2}} , \frac{x}{x^{2} + y^{2}} \rangle$$ models the flow of a fluid. Show that if you drop a leaf into this fluid, as the leaf moves over time, the leaf does not rotate.
Is it possible for $$\mathbf{\text{G}} ( x , y , z ) = \langle \text{sin} x , \text{cos} y , \text{sin} ( x y z ) \rangle$$ to be the curl of a vector field?
Is it possible for function $$f ( x , y ) = x^{2} - y^{2} + x$$ to be the potential function of an electrostatic field located in a region of $$\RR^{2}$$ free of static charge?
